meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your startup. Learn how to optimize SaaS pricing and ship your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code to finally cross the finish line.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of revenue-driven constraints. By implementing a high-velocity pricing strategy early, you force market validation and feature prioritization. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing architecture and Claude Code to automate the deployment, transforming your stalled prototype into a profitable, production-ready SaaS engine.
content.intro:
You have a graveyard of half-finished repositories, each a monument to 'perfect' code that never touched a paying customer. The cycle is familiar: you obsess over architecture, scope creep sets in, and the motivation wanes because the product isn't solving a real business problem. You aren't failing because you lack skill; you are failing because you lack the forcing function of commerce.

True velocity comes from pricing your product before it is finished. When you commit to a subscription model, the code stops being a hobby and starts being a commitment. This guide leverages Antigravity and Claude Code to strip away the vanity metrics and force your project into a lean, revenue-generating MVP.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Stop charging for features and start charging for outcomes. Map your MVP features to a tiered pricing structure that forces you to prioritize the 'must-have' functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my core feature set and propose three SaaS pricing tiers based on value-based metrics. Ensure the entry-level tier solves the user's primary pain point to drive immediate conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a pricing-table.tsx component based on the value-based tiers identified.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Billing Infrastructure
steps.description: Integration complexity often kills projects. Use your tools to generate a clean, Stripe-ready backend architecture that handles subscriptions without bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a secure Stripe webhook handler and subscription management service using TypeScript. Focus on idempotency and error handling for failed payments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement stripe-webhook-handler.ts and integrate with the existing database schema.'

steps.title: Automate Feature Gating
steps.description: Prevent scope creep by linking every new line of code to a specific pricing tier. If a feature doesn't justify a tier, it doesn't get built.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware decorator for my API routes that restricts access based on the user's active subscription tier stored in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the subscription-guard middleware to all routes in /api/v1/features/*'

steps.title: Build the Revenue Dashboard
steps.description: Seeing your first dollar is the best anti-procrastination tool. Build a simple dashboard that tracks MRR and churn.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lightweight analytics view that pulls real-time subscription data from Stripe to display Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) and active users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create dashboard-stats.tsx and connect it to the Stripe API using server-side fetching.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Move from local dev to production immediately. Don't wait for perfection—wait for feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my build configuration for production readiness. Suggest the most efficient deployment path for a SaaS MVP to minimize latency and cost.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute production-ready build and verify environment variables for Stripe integration.'

pro_tips: Always price your lowest tier at a point that covers your server costs plus a 20% margin to ensure the project is self-sustaining.
Use 'Feature Flagging' to hide unfinished work; never let a lack of a complete feature stop you from deploying the core product.
Implement a 'Founder's Lifetime Deal' for your first 10 users to generate immediate cash flow and high-fidelity feedback.
Never build an admin panel until you have at least three paying customers; manual database updates are faster than building UI.
Automate your onboarding email sequence early; it is more important for retention than the aesthetic of your dashboard.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, focus exclusively on 'The First 10'—manually reach out to potential users and convert them to paid subscribers to validate your pricing model.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for your customers. You have the tools, the strategy, and the architecture to dominate your niche. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
